expectations.text: Asks at least one clarifying question OR explicitly states an assumption about the audience/situation
expectations.evidence: Reframes the audience explicitly ('she is not skeptical of analytics — she is skeptical of adoption'), offers to pressure-test the user's existing deck.

expectations.text: Names a specific story arc or structure and explains why it fits
expectations.evidence: Suggests a 30-minute time-blocked structure but does not invoke any named story arc or named structural device.

expectations.text: Addresses the VP's prior-bad-experience skepticism directly (does not paper over it)
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'her real question isn't is your product good?' — explicitly recenters the conversation on adoption skepticism.

expectations.text: Recommends using the pilot customers as social proof or named-brand evidence
expectations.evidence: 'Pilot proof, told as stories' section, plus 'get the names of the three tools she bought' as a reconnaissance move.

expectations.text: Produces a structured artifact (outline, scene list, beat sequence) rather than a wall of prose
expectations.evidence: Time-blocked 30-minute structure with clear section breaks; preparation checklist; objections to rehearse.

expectations.text: Ends with a revision handle: a question, an ask for input, or a 'here's what only you can fill in' callout
expectations.evidence: 'Good luck Tuesday. If you want, share your current deck or talking points and I'll pressure-test them.'

summary: 5/6 — strong general advice, but no named story arc and no explicit narrative tactics. This is the central diff vs. with_skill.
